summary: Gruber announces a new Daring Fireball t-shirt design by illustrator Kevin Cornell and a new partnership with Oddica for printing and shipping fulfillment. The partnership aims to fix chronic delays in shirt delivery caused by Gruber's own poor inventory management. He acknowledges an embarrassingly large backlog of pending orders and support emails, promising all existing orders will ship before new ones. Both the new 'Daredevil Helmet' design and the classic DF logo shirt will be printed on American Apparel blanks and begin shipping in mid-April.
key_points: New 'Daredevil Helmet' t-shirt design by illustrator Kevin Cornell, who will share in profits
Outsourcing fulfillment to Oddica to replace Gruber's dysfunctional self-managed shipping process
Honest admission that previous order fulfillment took weeks or months due to his own mistakes
All pending backlogged orders promised to ship before new orders begin in mid-April
money_quotes: In a certain sense, I've wanted this t-shirt since I was about four years old.

This should take a process that previously took weeks — and in some cases, due to my own mistakes and poor inventory management, months — and turn it into a process that takes just a few days to get new shirts into your hands.
I have an embarrassingly large queue of support email regarding delayed and lost orders.
Expect this site to be relatively quiet for the next day or two as I work through these piles.
key_insight: Gruber's willingness to publicly own his fulfillment failures and outsource to specialists reflects the same pragmatism he champions in software — recognize what you're bad at and delegate it.
